Ad Text:
Our purpose

Here at Datacom, we connect people and technology in order to solve challenges, create opportunities and discover new possibilities for the communities we live in.

About the Role

We’re looking for people that want the challenges of working in an MNC with diverse clients, high paced development using the latest technologies and techniques and delivering high quality applications that make our clients happy. We want people passionate about delivering innovative software. If you’ve already achieved great results in your career and want to extend to new clients, teams, and tools then this is probably the right opportunity for you.

We’re creating a team of motivated, ambitious people that want to show the world what they can do by creating great applications for customers. You’ll have access to great technologies, great people and opportunities and the backing of one of the region’s premier IT companies. Join us to find out how you can take advantage of these opportunities and take your career to the next level.

We’re looking for a confident Technical Business Analyst able to work in an agile (Scrum) team helping guide the use of technology and delivery approach. You will have strong technical skills (particularly on Integration focused projects) acting as the pivot or intermediary between end-users and IT (Information Technology) to ensure that business/user requirements are delivered in IT solutions.

What you’ll do:

Requirements Discovery

Understand the client's business, existing processes, problems, and needs you will be responsible for

Creating JIRA tickets
Acceptance criteria
Implementation details
Mappings of consumers and providers
Document information on Confluence
UML diagrams (sequence), Integration design patterns
Mapping tables
Endpoints and environments
Postman collections
Acceptance criteria
Facilitate workshops and discussion sessions/channels
3 amigos session (Dev. tester and BA)
Data model implementation
HTTP Operations
Data types
Technical Ability:
Create Swagger Specifications (Contracts) - Swagger Editor or Notepad++ or VS code
Understanding of the integration patterns and flows:
SOAP vs REST APIs
API contract design and maintenance
Consumers and providers of the APIs
Error Handling patterns
Messaging Queuing Processes such as ActiveMQ, Kafka
Estimation and Team Management – You will provide estimates for Change Requests and larger pieces of work following a reliable and traceable estimation methodology.
Design the system from a functional perspective, produce drafts of the Design documents, lead/manage the review of those documents with the client, and achieve approval and signoff of the Design documents
Work closely with the Technical Lead and Architects, ensure they are kept updated, and especially made aware of scope changes, project issues, and risks
Provide team with business experience, advice on industry best practices, etc.
Provide input into User Guides as required
Ensure all reviews required are completed at the required checkpoints

Tools Experience:

Confluence/Jira
Splunk
Swagger
Postman (API testing)
SOAP UI
Intellij/eclipse
Git/Bitbucket

Professional Qualities:

5+ years of experience as a Technical Business Analyst working in Agile teams.
Attention to detail
Deadline driven
Excellent communication skills
Work well within a team environment
Proficient in English

Responsibilities

As a Business Analyst you’ll be responsible for the following day-to-day activities:

Produce drafts of the Requirements documents, lead/manage the review of those documents with the client, achieve approval and signoff of the Requirements documents
Lead the Analysis and Design stage, arrange and lead any communication, meetings, and workshops for this stage
Work closely with the Technical Lead, ensuring they have sufficient understanding of the functional requirements to design the System Architecture.
Work closely with the Test Analysts, ensure they are also kept updated, and especially made aware of scope changes, project issues, and risks
Resolve issues raised by developers and testers during the Development and Testing stages, in relation to specification questions and ambiguities, communicating with clients wherever necessary

About Datacom

We have over 6,200 people across our global offices, and generate an annual revenue of over $1.2 billion, this makes us one of Australasia’s largest professional IT services companies. We have extensive expertise in operating data centres, providing IT services, software engineering and application management, as well as payroll and customer service design and operations. With this comes a long list of significant clients. Datacom is committed to hiring, developing and promoting the best talent from a diverse range of backgrounds. We are local at heart, yet world-class in capability.

Job Title: Senior FP&A Analyst | $130k - $150k | PE Backed Healthcare
Ad Text:
PE Backed Healthcare Business | Senior FP&A Analyst Role
High Growth Environment | Strategic & Commercial Focus
$130k - $150k + STI | Sydney CBD Fringe
The Opportunity
Reporting to the Head of FP&A, this role offers a excellent opportunity to join a growing, PE-backed healthcare business. You will play a central role in financial planning, analysis, and strategic decision support across a national network of clinics.
This position suits a candidate coming from Big 4 deals, transaction services, or a strong analytical finance background, looking to step into a commercial, business-facing role. You will work closely with senior leadership, providing clear insights that shape performance, investment decisions, and growth strategy.
The business has strong funding, an active acquisition pipeline, and a clear mandate for expansion. You will gain exposure to board-level reporting, M&A activity, and complex financial modelling in a fast-moving environment.

The Challenge
Your key responsibilities will include but are not limited to:
Leading the annual budgeting process and maintaining rolling forecasts, delivering variance analysis and board-ready commentary
Partnering with operational leaders to assess performance, identify trends, and drive commercial outcomes
Building business cases to evaluate new clinics, partnerships, and strategic growth initiatives
Developing and maintaining KPI dashboards and executive reporting packs
Supporting and leading financial due diligence on acquisitions, including quality of earnings and EBITDA analysis
Owning integrated 3-statement financial models and cash flow forecasts to support planning and liquidity management
Delivering scenario and sensitivity analysis to assess risk and support decision making
Driving continuous improvement across financial processes, reporting, and systems
The Expertise
Please consider expressing interest in this position if you possess the following:

CA / CPA qualified, or working towards completion
4+ years’ experience in FP&A, corporate finance, transaction services, or a related analytical role
Background in Big 4 or a commercial finance team within industry
Strong financial modelling capability, including exposure to 3 way models and scenario analysis
Proven experience in budgeting, forecasting, and performance analysis within a complex environment
Advanced Excel skills; exposure to Power BI or similar tools highly regarded
Ability to translate complex financial data into clear, concise insights for non-finance stakeholders
Experience in M&A, due diligence, or investment analysis will be highly regarded
The Next Step
If this position will enhance your career and you would like further details on the client, the role, remuneration or a copy of the position description, please call Henry Clunies-Ross for a confidential discussion on 0477 359 020 or alternatively please apply per the link.

Melbourne – Wurundjeri Country, Australia
Senior Technical Delivery Manager


At AKQA (part of WPP), our Technology team design and deliver digital products and platforms that define how brands connect with customers. Increasingly, this includes AI-powered experiences that require new ways of thinking, building, and delivering. As a Senior Technical Delivery Manager, you will play a key role in evolving how we work, helping teams adopt new tools, workflows, and AI-enabled delivery practices as part of a rapidly changing technology landscape.

In this role, you will lead the end-to-end delivery of complex digital and AI-enabled products, working closely with engineers, architects, and designers to bring ambitious ideas to life. You will be responsible for ensuring solutions are technically sound, scalable, and delivered to a high standard, balancing delivery rigour with a strong focus on product quality and real-world impact. 

As part of AKQA, you’ll work in an innovative and inclusive culture, surrounded by some of the brightest minds in their fields. You’ll collaborate within a creative and technically advanced team, helping shape modern delivery practices in an evolving technology landscape. Our hybrid in-studio/WFH model supports balance and flexibility.

ROLE REQUIREMENTS

Lead end-to-end delivery of complex technical and AI-enabled products.
Ensure solutions are well-architected, scalable, and built to a high standard.
Work closely with development, architecture, and design teams to shape and deliver product outcomes.
Translate product and technical requirements into clear, actionable delivery plans.
Manage scope, timelines, and budgets while maintaining a strong focus on product quality.
Identify and manage risks, dependencies, and trade-offs across projects.
Drive collaboration across multidisciplinary teams and stakeholders.
Contribute to evolving delivery practices, including adoption of AI tools and new ways of working.

QUALITIES & CHARACTERISTICS

Strong experience delivering complex digital products in a technical environment.
Understand modern architectures (e.g. composable, headless, cloud-based).
Experience with Agile delivery and iterative product development.
Ability to balance delivery discipline with product thinking and quality outcomes.
Confident stakeholder management and communication skills.
Proactive, adaptable, and comfortable operating in a fast-changing environment.
Strong problem-solving skills, with the ability to navigate complexity and bring clarity.
A genuine interest in AI and emerging technologies, and how they shape product delivery.
A focus on quality and impact, not just timelines and outputs.
Ability to challenge thinking constructively to improve outcomes.
Leads by example, bringing energy, ownership, and momentum to delivery.

We believe great work happens when we're together, fostering creativity, collaboration, and connection. That's why we’ve adopted a hybrid approach including required in-office days. If you require accommodations or flexibility, please discuss this with the hiring team during the interview process.

AKQA is an equal opportunity employer and considers applicants for all positions without discrimination or regard to particular characteristics. We are committed to fostering a culture of respect in which everyone feels they belong and has the same opportunities to progress in their careers.

About WPP

WPP is the trusted growth partner for the world’s leading brands. We unite cutting-edge media intelligence and data solutions, world-class creativity, next-generation production, transformative enterprise solutions and expert strategic counsel in a single company – powered by exceptional talent and our agentic marketing platform, WPP Open, to help our clients navigate change, capture opportunity and deliver transformational growth. For more information, visit WPP.com.

This is an excellent opportunity for an energetic, dynamic Senior Commercial Analyst to join a successful, global construction group.

Reporting into the CFO you'll act as a business partner to operational stakeholders, providing commercial, financial & analytical support, to help drive decisions & business performance.

Your analytical scope will encompass areas such as category sales, discounting, gross margins, pre & post promotional analysis and wages. You'll create financial models in order to provide meaningful insights into business performance issues, challenging decisions where necessary.

You'll also prepare and distribute weekly & monthly reports, with associated commentary, for use by the business. And will hold regular meetings with Category Managers and the Commercial Director, to review monthly sales, margin results etc.

Additionally, you'll play a key role in business planning for new stores, budgeting, forecasting and other ad hoc analysis when required.

You are an experienced Financial or Commercial Analyst - who will be ready to step into a Commercial Manager role in the next 1-2 years. Circa 5 years experience in Commercial Finance role/s would be ideal. This role has 1 direct report.

You'll have proven analytical skills, combined with a background in business partnering. You will also have advanced Excel / modelling skills, whilst experience using a BI tool and working with large data sets.

For more information, please contact Schenuk Wijesekera on 02 9775 5916. Or to apply, hit the "apply" button.
